Summary: [IT-3022274] Request for Data Validation: Claim Raw/model vs NILE Raw Data (Claims Header Fields) Created: 29/Apr/26 EDT Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT
Description: Client: BCBS_ND We are validating data in the ARK platform and observed that all fields in the Claims Header are populated from the Claim model, which in turn is sourced from Claim raw data. The values in the Claim model and raw data match exactly with the Claims Header, indicating that the data is loading as per the current logic. Since the Claim model data originates from NILE, we would like to understand how the raw data from NILE is structured and whether any transformations are applied to the relevant columns before being delivered downstream. Currently, we do not have visibility into the NILE raw files/tables to verify this. For example members CH_PATIENT_CLIENT_MASTER_ID (member_id) IN (135557853, 66442765, 93553925), OPS has requested validation of the raw data to determine whether the client is sending multiple policy/UMI plans per member. The goal is to identify whether the root cause of the mapping issue is related to how the client sends the data or a Cotiviti mapping issue. The columns to validate in the Claims Header are [PatNum] , [PatFirstName] , [PatLastName] , [EmployerName] , and [GroupNum] , with corresponding Claim Model/Raw columns CH_PATIENT_CLIENT_ID, CH_PATIENT_FIRST_NAME, CH_PATIENT_LAST_NAME, CH_GROUP_NAME, and CH_GROUP_NUMBER.

Summary: [IT-3020658] NAS Spoolers failed and pipeline not completed,Hue browser not working in production environment. Created: 29/Apr/26 EDT Updated: 29/Apr/26 EDT Resolved: 29/Apr/26 EDT
Description: Today, we experienced a Hadoop issue where spoolers failed, pipelines got stuck, and the Hue browser became inaccessible.
Comment: The HUE browser is not accessible, and from the Nile side all NAS spoolers have failed. The failure occurred while trying to write files to HDFS. The error indicates that the HDFS NameNode is in safe mode, which prevents any file creation or write operations. Because of this, the spooler is unable to write files to the HDFS path, causing all spooling processes to fail. The system will remain in safe mode until the required block threshold is met, after which it should automatically turn off and resume normal operations. Hadoop noticed the issue and rolling restart the cluster. Instatly issue was resolved. After sometime pipeline are not running as expected. we notify and connected with different teams. They noticed the issue and resolved with the cluster restart. Summary: [IT-2982754] Please Create Pipeline dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators_Pipe Created: 10/Apr/26 EDT Updated: 17/Apr/26 EDT Resolved: 17/Apr/26 EDT
Description: Hello Nile Team. Please create a new pipeline - dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators_Pipe This will serve as a alternate pipeline to existing pipeline dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_ING_HCPCSIndicators . This new pipeline will have a source file as pipe-delimited with a header record. The file must be loaded in Append mode, since data already exists in the SQL table. The DDDL for the SQL table is attached. Here are some notes on the columns: The BETOS field is not mapped to SQL The EffectiveDt column is taken from the name of the folder containing the source file and is the first day of the calendar quarter (i.e., YYYY-01-01 YYYY-04-01 YYYY-07-01 YYYY-10-01) For example if the folder is Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\Ingenix\HCPCS\2026-04-01 then the EffectiveDt is 2026-04-01. The EndDt column is the last day of the quarter being loaded (i.e., if EffectiveDt = YYYY-01-01 then EndDt = YYYY-03-31 if EffectiveDt = YYYY-04-01 then EndDt = YYYY-06-30 if EffectiveDt = YYYY-07-01 then EndDt = YYYY-09-30 if EffectiveDt = YYYY-10-01 then EndDt = YYYY-12-31) The file name pattern is OPTUM_HCPCS_INDICATORS_YYYY_MM_pipe.txt
